# Teams Channel

## Overview

Microsoft Teams channel ‚Äî webhook handler bridging Azure Bot Service activities to MCP-driven conversations.

The Teams handler is exposed by the ``backend_api`` service at ``POST /api/v1/channels/teams/{profile_id}/messages``. Each organisation registers an Azure App + Bot resource and stores ``app_id`` / ``app_password`` / ``tenant_id`` in ``interface_config``. Inbound activities are JWT-validated against the Microsoft Bot Framework OpenID metadata before being dispatched.

## Quick facts

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Interface key | `teams` |
| Config storage | `interface_profile.interface_config` |
| CLI module | `src.ops_cli.channels.teams` |
| Worker modules | `src.backend_api.app.api.v1.channels_teams`, `src.teams_handler.handler` |
| Webhook paths | `POST /api/v1/channels/teams/{profile_id}/messages` |

## Per-organisation configuration

> The JSON below describes the per-organisation configuration. It is stored in `GSageInterfaceProfile.interface_config` (JSONB column) and managed via `python -m ops_cli channels teams ‚Ä¶`.

> **Sensitive fields** (passwords, tokens, secrets) are currently stored *as-is* in the JSONB column ‚Äî gSage does not yet encrypt them at rest. Treat the database as a secret store and provide values through the CLI's `--*-stdin` flags so they never appear in argv or shell history.

### Empty template (required fields only)

```json
{
  "app_id": "<value>",
  "app_password": "<value>",
  "tenant_id": "<value>"
}
```

### Example payload

```json
{
  "app_id": "<value>",
  "app_password": "<value>",
  "tenant_id": "<value>"
}
```

### Field reference

| Field | Type | Required | Sensitive | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | :---: | :---: | --- | --- |
| `app_id` | `string` | ‚úì | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | Azure App Registration (client) ID. |
| `app_password` | `string` | ‚úì | ‚úì | ‚Äî | Azure App client secret. |
| `tenant_id` | `string` | ‚úì | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | Azure tenant ID (used for Microsoft Graph lookups when resolving AAD users to emails). |


## Environment variables

These cluster-wide variables seed the channel's runtime defaults. Set them in your `.env` (or via your secrets backend) before starting the worker. **Per-org configuration** (see the previous section) takes precedence at runtime.

| Variable | Type | Default | Sensitive | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | :---: | --- |
| `TEAMS_BOT_OPENID_METADATA_URL` | `string` | `https://login.botframework.com/v1/.well-known/openidconfiguration` | ‚Äî | Microsoft Bot Framework public OpenID configuration URL ‚Äî override only for sovereign / gov clouds. |
| `TEAMS_GRAPH_EMAIL_CACHE_TTL` | `integer` | `86400` | ‚Äî | Cache TTL (s) for AAD-Object-ID ‚Üí email lookups via Microsoft Graph (first-contact resolution only). |
| `TEAMS_MAX_MESSAGE_LENGTH` | `integer` | `25000` | ‚Äî | Outbound chunk size before splitting (Teams hard cap on a single text activity is 28 KB). |
| `TEAMS_RATE_LIMIT_ORG_DAILY` | `integer` | `200` | ‚Äî | Maximum activities forwarded per organisation per UTC day. |
| `TEAMS_RATE_LIMIT_USER_HOURLY` | `integer` | `30` | ‚Äî | Maximum activities forwarded per Teams user per rolling hour. |

## CLI usage

**Create / update the org-wide Teams profile**

```bash
echo '<client-secret>' | python -m ops_cli channels teams upsert \
    --org-slug gsage \
    --description "Main SOC bot" \
    --app-id 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 \
    --tenant-id 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 \
    --app-password-stdin
```

**Inspect the current profile (secret redacted)**

```bash
python -m ops_cli channels teams show --org-slug gsage
```

**Delete the profile**

```bash
python -m ops_cli channels teams delete --org-slug gsage --yes
```


## Prerequisites

- An Azure AD App Registration with a client secret.
- An Azure Bot resource whose **messaging endpoint** points at `https://<your-host>/api/v1/channels/teams/<profile_id>/messages`.
- The `Microsoft Teams` channel enabled on the Azure Bot resource.
- Microsoft Graph delegated permission `User.Read.All` granted to the App Registration (used for AAD-Object-ID ‚Üí email lookups).
- The `backend_api` service must be reachable from the Bot Framework.
